user_query:
I’m a parish council member in Colombo reviewing our March newsletter draft. Please critique it for alignment with our parish communication guidelines and consistency with the planned event schedule. Use only the local workspace files provided and produce structured outputs.

Inputs (all paths are relative to the workspace):
- input/newsletter.html
- input/schedule.csv
- input/guidelines.yaml
- input/calendar.json

Required outputs:
1) output/critique.json
   - Fields:
     - guideline_violations: list of objects { rule: string, evidence: string, location: string }
       • Check for banned phrases from input/guidelines.yaml (case-insensitive), and flag any political endorsements.
       • Check whether any fundraising-related item falls within the Lent period defined in input/calendar.json. Evidence may come from the events list or dated announcements in input/newsletter.html.
     - schedule_mismatches: list of objects { title: string, field: "date"|"time"|"location", newsletter_value: string, schedule_value: string } and/or { title: string, type: "missing_from_schedule"|"missing_from_newsletter" }
       • Extract events from input/newsletter.html (title, date, time, location) and compare against input/schedule.csv, matching titles by exact string equality after trimming. Report any differences and any items present in one source but not the other.
     - broken_links: list of objects { href: string, context: string, error_message: string }
       • For every relative link (href not starting with http) found in input/newsletter.html, run a shell command that attempts to stat the path relative to the workspace root (e.g., a standard OS utility). Save raw command output (including any errors) to output/link_check.log. Derive broken_links from the actual command stderr/error messages rather than from inference.
     - overall_judgment: string summarizing whether the draft is acceptable as-is or needs changes.
     - recommendations: list of concise, actionable suggestions.

2) output/extracted_events.csv
   - Columns: title,date,time,location
   - Populate from the events embedded in input/newsletter.html (use data attributes and the visible "Location:" text).

3) output/link_check.log
   - The raw, line-by-line output collected from the link existence checks for each relative href.

Constraints and notes:
- Do not use the Internet; rely only on the provided inputs.
- Make the link-check step essential: analyze the actual command output or error messages to determine broken_links. If no errors occur, broken_links should be an empty list.
- Treat Lent boundaries as inclusive of both start_date and end_date from input/calendar.json.
- Consider fundraising references via event titles and/or dated text in the announcements section; include an evidence snippet showing the date within Lent.
- Ensure all output files are created exactly at the specified paths.

Deliverables: output/critique.json, output/extracted_events.csv, output/link_check.log. If you need to produce intermediate files, keep them under output/.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
